title: Fix Cross-Tenant Taxonomy Value Read via IDOR
description:
**Penetration Test Finding — Medium Severity**

An admin of Tenant A could read taxonomy values of all tenants due to missing access control on the taxonomy values endpoint. Taxonomy IDs were only 2 digits and could be brute-forced using Burp Suite Intruder or Hydra to enumerate all tenants' taxonomy values.

## Affected Endpoint

`/metrics/get-selected-taxonomy-values/`

## Risk

Medium — confidentiality of tenant-specific taxonomy data was compromised. Simple brute-force of 2-digit IDs made enumeration trivial.

## Fix

Added the `UsedByTeam` trait to the taxonomy values model to scope queries to the authenticated user's team. Additionally recommended migrating from sequential 2-digit IDs to UUIDs application-wide.
acceptance_criteria: Taxonomy values endpoint returns only values belonging to the authenticated user's team
Cross-tenant taxonomy ID requests are rejected with 403 Forbidden
Legitimate same-tenant taxonomy value lookups continue to work
Brute-force enumeration of taxonomy IDs no longer returns cross-tenant data
middleware_changes: Added `UsedByTeam` trait to the taxonomy values model, automatically scoping all queries to the authenticated user's team.
api_changes: POST /metrics/get-selected-taxonomy-values/ now enforces tenant-scoped access control; returns 403 for cross-tenant requests.
implementation_notes: The root cause was a missing `UsedByTeam` trait on the taxonomy values model. This Laravel trait auto-scopes queries by the authenticated user's team_id. The pen test also recommended migrating from 2-digit sequential IDs to UUIDs across the application.
comment_summary: Developer identified the missing UsedByTeam trait as the root cause and applied it to the taxonomy values model. Deployed to staging.
